hero.title: Hairspray
hero.reason: In honor of National Almond Butter Crunch Day, let's get crunching with a fun and upbeat musical like Hairspray, which features a plethora of energetic dance numbers and a vibrant atmosphere that's sure to get you moving.
hero.blurb: With its star-studded cast, including John Travolta and Queen Latifah, Hairspray is a wildly entertaining ride that will keep you smiling from start to finish. Its catchy musical numbers and impressive choreography make it a must-see for fans of the genre.

released.title: Ratatouille
released.reason: Released today in 2007, Ratatouille remains a beloved animated classic that continues to delight audiences with its stunning animation and charming storyline.
released.blurb: This beautifully crafted film tells the story of a talented rat named Remy who dreams of becoming a chef in a Parisian restaurant. With its stunning animation and lovable characters, Ratatouille is a must-see for fans of all ages.

birthday.title: The 'Burbs
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to Kathy Baker, born June 29, 1950, starring in this hilarious comedy about a suburban neighborhood turned upside down by a mysterious family.
birthday.blurb: With its talented cast, including Tom Hanks and Carrie Fisher, The 'Burbs is a wildly entertaining ride that will keep you laughing from start to finish. Its wacky humor and satirical take on suburban life make it a classic comedy of the 80s.

awful.title: I Know Who Killed Me
awful.reason: This infamous thriller starring Lindsay Lohan is often cited as one of the worst films of the 2000s, with a convoluted plot and poor acting that make it a fascinating trainwreck to watch.
awful.blurb: With its bizarre storyline and cringe-worthy performances, I Know Who Killed Me is a so-bad-it's-good masterpiece that will leave you questioning the sanity of its creators. It's a mesmerizingly bad film that's worth watching for its sheer audacity alone.
